methodsAttempted.notes: Primary domain: jhu.edu. Key subdomains: teaching.jhu.edu, engineering.jhu.edu, ep.jhu.edu, brand.jhu.edu, wseit.engineering.jhu.edu, ii.library.jhu.edu, imagine.jhu.edu, support.cmts.jhu.edu, guides.library.jhu.edu

OpenCLI Google search. Found: Teaching@JHU generative AI guidance and guidelines pages, JHU Press generative AI policy for authors (PDF), EP faculty-facing guidance, Engineering student use of generative AI, brand AI guidelines.
OpenCLI Google search. Found: Sheridan Library ChatGPT introduction, Engineering academic integrity page, Teaching@JHU resources.
OpenCLI Google search. Found: Teaching@JHU guidelines and syllabus statements, WSEIT generative AI/CoPilot info, CMTS AI tools guide, library AI research guide, brand AI page.
Cloudflare WAF blocks HTTP fetch. Search results confirm pages exist: /university-teaching-policies/generative-ai/ and /university-teaching-policies/generative-ai/guidelines/
Cloudflare WAF blocks HTTP fetch. Search results confirm: /ctld/chatgpt/ hub, student use page, academic integrity page, AI tools page.

JHU subdomains (teaching.jhu.edu, engineering.jhu.edu, ep.jhu.edu, press.jhu.edu) are behind Cloudflare WAF. HTTP fetch returns 403. These pages require browser-based access. wseit.engineering.jhu.edu and support.cmts.jhu.edu may also be blocked. Pages confirmed to exist via search results.

summary: JHU has comprehensive AI guidance distributed across multiple subdomains. Teaching@JHU (teaching.jhu.edu) is the central hub with generative AI tool guidance, detailed guidelines, and syllabus statements. Engineering (engineering.jhu.edu) has a dedicated ChatGPT/CTLD hub. JHU Press has a formal generative AI policy for authors (PDF). EP (ep.jhu.edu) has faculty-facing guidance. Most JHU subdomains are behind Cloudflare WAF, requiring browser-based access for crawl-worker. 6 verified sources identified, all marked needs_browser due to Cloudflare WAF.
